Title: PtrVINV2 is dispensable for cellulose synthesis but essential for salt tolerance in Populus trichocarpa Torr. and Gray

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Secondary xylem development; osmotic adjustment and salt tolerance
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing xylem; stem; leaves under salt stress
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Transcript positively correlated with cellulose content and negatively correlated with hemicellulose, lignin and starch contents in prior correlation analysis
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed mainly in developing xylem using the DX15 promoter, with minimal changes in leaves and roots
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes lignin accumulation and reduces hemicellulose accumulation without affecting cellulose content; promotes secondary xylem development; enhances salt tolerance by promoting sucrose hydrolysis into glucose and fructose
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Developing xylem-specific overexpression; CRISPR/Cas9 knockout; qRT-PCR; subcellular localization in onion epidermis; invertase activity assays; structural and non-structural carbohydrate quantification; histochemical staining; transcriptome sequencing; WGCNA; salt stress phenotyping
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Developing xylem-specific overexpression; 200 mmol/L NaCl salt treatment for 10 days
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Knockout reduced lignin and increased hemicellulose, reduced xylem width/cell layers, and increased salt sensitivity; overexpression did not significantly increase structural carbohydrate content
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cellulose content remained stable in both knockout and overexpression contexts, indicating PtrVINV2 is dispensable for cellulose synthesis but important for lignin/hemicellulose balance and salt tolerance. KO-PtrVINV2-18 showed dwarfism/chlorosis likely due to T-DNA insertion effects and was excluded from later analyses.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PtrVINV2 encodes a vacuolar membrane-localized vacuolar invertase whose overexpression increases and knockout decreases VINV activity. 2. PtrVINV2 is not essential for cellulose synthesis but positively regulates lignin accumulation, negatively regulates hemicellulose accumulation, and promotes secondary xylem development. 3. PtrVINV2 is required for salt tolerance, likely by sustaining sucrose hydrolysis, osmotic adjustment, and antioxidant capacity under salinity.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for breeding or engineering salt-tolerant poplar with modified wood properties; tissue-specific manipulation may improve tolerance while minimizing whole-plant growth penalties.

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified as a hub transcription factor in the red module associated with starch, sucrose and CWINV activity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potential involvement in salt tolerance-related carbohydrate regulation in PtrVINV2 transgenic plants
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as osmotic regulation-related based on prior literature; no direct functional validation in this paper.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Potri.001G219100 was identified as a hub MYB transcription factor in a module positively correlated with starch, sucrose and CWINV activity. 2. The paper proposes it may participate in PtrVINV2-associated salt tolerance mechanisms. 3. No direct perturbation experiment for this gene was performed in this study.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate marker/gene for future engineering of salt tolerance networks in poplar.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Abiotic stress response; possible regulation of structural carbohydrate-associated salt tolerance traits
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified as a hub transcription factor in the turquoise module positively correlated with lignin and cellulose and negatively correlated with hemicellulose
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potential involvement in abiotic stress and structural carbohydrate regulatory network associated with PtrVINV2
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No direct functional validation in this paper.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Potri.009G106600 was identified as a hub G2-like transcription factor in a structural carbohydrate-associated coexpression module. 2. Prior literature links this family to abiotic stress responses. 3. The paper hypothesizes a role in PtrVINV2-mediated salt tolerance.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate regulatory node for combined wood-property and stress-tolerance improvement.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Salt tolerance and carbohydrate-associated stress adaptation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing xylem; leaves under salt stress
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Identified as a hub transcription factor in the yellow module positively correlated with hemicellulose and negatively correlated with lignin
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Potential regulator of salt tolerance and structural/non-structural carbohydrate balance in PtrVINV2 transgenic plants
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Function inferred from hub status and prior literature; not directly validated in this paper.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Potri.002G081000 was identified as a hub NAC transcription factor in a module associated with higher hemicellulose and lower lignin. 2. Prior studies suggest NAC family involvement in poplar salt tolerance. 3. The paper hypothesizes that it contributes to PtrVINV2-mediated salt tolerance mechanisms.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Candidate gene for future salt-tolerance engineering and network dissection in poplar.
